LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BARBARA CRANE'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 === AFTER HEARING THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FINAL. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL (ANTI-SLAPP) MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE CLAIMS ALLEGED IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DO NOT ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT IMPLICATE DEFENDANTS' PETITIONING ACTIVITIES AS IT ARISES FROM A CONTRACTUAL DISPUTE. ANY REFERENCE TO THE MARIN ACTION IS TANGENTIAL AND ATTENUATED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BARBARA CRANE'S AMENDED NOTICE OF DEMURRER TO AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND AMENDED DEMURRER IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 === AFTER HEARING THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FINAL. AS TO DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER, IT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT HIS CLAIM. DEFENDANT MUST FILE ITS ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER.
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-21-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Argued, DEFENDANT BARBARA CRANE'S Amended Notice Of Demurrer To And Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint AND Amended Demurrer is taken under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

============================================================================================
After hearing the court adopts its tentative ruling as final.
As to Defendants' demurrer, it is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support his claim. Defendant must file its answer within 10 days of notice of entry of this order.

AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter R. Crane, Esq., Crane Law Group, 415-302-6725, for defendant and moving party
Wayne Lewis Lesser, Esq., Attorney at Law, 415-563-2111, for Plaintiff and opposing party
Vivian V. Countryman, Esq., Hayes, Scott, Bonino, Ellingson & McLay, LLP, 650-637-9100, for Cross-defendant Godrey Tencer

Argued, DEFENDANT BARBARA CRANE'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL (ANTI-SLAPP) MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is taken under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
==========================================================================================
After hearing the court adopts its tentative ruling as final.
DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL (ANTI-SLAPP) MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED.
THE CLAIMS ALLEGED IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DO NOT ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY.
The court finds that the amended complaint does not implicate Defendants' petitioning activities as it arises from a contractual dispute. Any reference to the Marin action is tangential and attenuated.
